ActivInspire documentation and file.org verified that .flipchart files are ZIP archives containing XML with optional .FLP alternative extension

pi_items.verified_value: Atoms prefer 8 electrons in valence shell (noble gas configuration). Exceptions: H prefers 2 (duplet rule), He is stable with 2, and period 3+ elements can have expanded octets.
pi_items.source: Chemistry LibreTexts and Wikipedia definitions of Octet Rule. Task description correctly specifies to mention '8 valence electrons' in Octet Rule definition.

privileged_info_summary: The Lewis Dot Structure lesson task requires creating a 4-page ActivInspire flipchart saved to /home/ga/Documents/Flipcharts/lewis_dot_structures.flipchart. Page 1 includes title 'Lewis Dot Structures', text 'Covalent Bonding', and Octet Rule definition mentioning 8 valence electrons. Page 2 titled 'Valence Electrons' shows element symbols (H, C, N, O, and Cl) with dots representing valence electrons. Page 3 'Water (H2O)' demonstrates Lewis structure with H and O. Page 4 'Practice' lists three molecules (CO2, NH3, CH4). The task requires minimum 10 shapes (electron dots) and specific chemistry content verified against authoritative sources. DISCREPANCY FOUND: Metadata lists only 4 required elements [H, C, N, O] but task description specifies 5 elements including Cl.
